node.statement: (Contrapositive for infinite-dimensional case). If π_φ(A)' is infinite-dimensional (i.e., dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) = ∞), then φ cannot be an extreme point of any set C defined by finitely many affine constraints. Equivalently, under finitely many constraints, every extreme point has a finite-dimensional commutant and the multiplicity bound applies. This is the useful form for infinite-dimensional algebras where infinite-dimensional commutants exist.
